subject: FW: BLACK MAGIC
body:
From: Dan Klores [mailto:dan_klores@dkcnews.com] 
Sent: Thursday, January 10, 2008 12:41 PM
To: Dan Klores
Subject: BLACK MAGIC

 

Here's an "offer" that absolutely will not cost you one penny. On Monday
evening February 25th, my film "Black Magic", a story of the injustice
that defines the Civil Rights Movement told through the lives of
basketball players and coaches who attended Historically Black Colleges
only will have a fun premiere event at The Apollo Theater. The Chairman
of ESPN, Congressman Rangel and Earl Monroe, one of my producers are
hosting the evening. The film is narrated by Samuel L. Jackson and
Wynton Marsalis.

            

This should be a special evening in New York City with a great and
diverse mix of people. Your invitation should arrive in 10 days. I'm
writing to you in order to ask if we can list your name on the printed
invitation as one of the 200 "committee members". I repeat, the evening
is for free and you will never be asked for any other contribution
whatsoever. 

 

The only thing I can guarantee is that if the projector doesn't break
down you'll be proud of the film, and you and your guest will enjoy the
evening.

            

To make matters easier please just respond to this email with a YES.

 

Thanks, hope all is well.

 

Cordially,

Dan
body_html:
From: Dan Klores [mailto:dan_klores@dkcnews.com]
Sent: Thursday, January 10, 2008 12:41 PM
To: Dan Klores
Subject: BLACK MAGIC

 

Here’s an “offer” that absolutely will not cost you one penny. On Monday evening February 25th, my film “Black Magic”, a story of the injustice that defines the Civil Rights Movement told through the lives of basketball players and coaches who attended Historically Black Colleges only will have a fun premiere event at The Apollo Theater. The Chairman of ESPN, Congressman Rangel and Earl Monroe, one of my producers are hosting the evening. The film is narrated by Samuel L. Jackson and Wynton Marsalis.

           

This should be a special evening in New York City with a great and diverse mix of people. Your invitation should arrive in 10 days. I’m writing to you in order to ask if we can list your name on the printed invitation as one of the 200 “committee members”. I repeat, the evening is for free and you will never be asked for any other contribution whatsoever.

 

The only thing I can guarantee is that if the projector doesn’t break down you’ll be proud of the film, and you and your guest will enjoy the evening.

           

To make matters easier please just respond to this email with a YES.

 

Thanks, hope all is well.

 

Cordially,

Dan
